population: Critically ill adults admitted to ICUs for at least 24 hours in tertiary hospitals in China
sample_size: N=4,910 (2,086 with sepsis or septic shock)
timing: On ICU admission or within first 48 hours
notes: ICU mortality varied according to geographical region. Acinetobacter, Pseudomonas, and Staphylococcus infections associated with increased ICU mortality. Age, APACHE II scores, pre-existing cardiovascular diseases, malignant tumors, and renal replacement therapy also identified as risk factors.

population: ICU patients in Chinese tertiary hospitals with sepsis or septic shock
predictor: Prompt administration of antibiotics and sufficient fluid resuscitation
notes: More than two-fifths of patients developed sepsis on admission or within 48h of ICU admission; more than half had septic shock; in-hospital mortality in septic shock patients was 44.4%; Acinetobacter and Pseudomonas were the most common isolated microorganisms

population: ICU patients in Chinese tertiary hospitals with septic shock
notes: Patients who stayed in ICU for less than 24h were excluded; sepsis prevalence was more than two-fifths of ICU admissions

population: ICU patients with sepsis and septic shock
effect_size: 1 organ failure: ICU mortality 9.9%; 4 or more organ failures: ICU mortality 60.8%
notes: Overall ICU mortality 17.0%; in-hospital mortality 21.5%. Sepsis ICU mortality 27.2%; sepsis in-hospital mortality 33.0%. Septic shock ICU mortality 39.0%; septic shock in-hospital mortality 44.4%.
